page: Microsoft Azure E128as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Easv7 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. The configuration features 64 physical cores and 128 vCPUs via hyperthreading, supported by 1024.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache sizes, alongside hardware support for AVX-512 vector extensions, cryptography acceleration, and virtualization via AMD SVM.

In synthetic and operational benchmarks, the E128as_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance. Multi-core CPU execution via stress-ng reaches 234061.17, while single-core performance registers at 3782.89. Memory subsystem capabilities are highlighted by a read-only small-block cached bandwidth of 12383.33 GB/sec and a large-block uncached write bandwidth of 199.07 GB/sec. Database operations and key-value store performance are notable, with Redis SET operations achieving 177.27 million operations per second and Passmark database operations scoring 40257.01.

This server type does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external Premium Storage options, and workloads requiring hardware graphics acceleration are not supported. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of 1024.0 GB of RAM paired with 128 dedicated vCPUs provides a balanced resource profile for memory-intensive enterprise applications without the overhead of local disk maintenance. The hardware profile and benchmark results indicate suitability for large-scale database management systems, in-memory caching layers, heavy multi-threaded decompression tasks, and high-throughput static web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E128as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Easv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores and is equipped with 1024.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and SHA_NI. Benchmark results place this server in the top tier, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 234061.17 and Redis SET throughput of 177.27 million operations per second. Tradeoffs include the absence of local storage and GPU hardware, requiring external Premium Storage. This configuration is engineered for memory-intensive workloads, such as large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and high-throughput data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E128as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 128 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, 1024.0 GB of RAM, and top-tier database and multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E128as_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processors for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and one terabyte of RAM for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores.
Equipped with 1024.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.
Delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core processing performance in benchmarks.
Lacks local storage and GPU hardware, relying on external Premium Storage.
Optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and heavy data decompression.
